project: kcl/fpjson — port of FPC fcl-json data model (TJSONData/TJSONObject/TJSONArray) + parser/serializer to bash
status: PLANNED (DRAFT) — plan + ledger only, no implementation. Roadmap position 7/7 — the strategic epic. PLAN IS DRAFT until the P0 owner review resolves two architecture forks: node representation (recommended: document instance + integer handles) and parser engine (recommended: pure-bash first, optional awk-coprocess accelerator later). Same owner-decision protocol as tdictionary API v2.
notes: Single source of truth for fpjson status. Plan: kcl/fpjson/PLAN.md. Source: fcl-json/src/fpjson.pp (4322 lines; TJSONData :126, number classes :222-393, TJSONString :395, TJSONBoolean :434, TJSONNull :468, TJSONArray :504, TJSONObject :634) + jsonscanner.pp (758) + jsonparser.pp (290). Seeds: fcl-json/tests (testjson.pp) + vendored JSONTestSuite subset (y_/n_/i_). Positioning: zero-fork JSON for configs and API payloads (kkbot lives among JSON APIs; today every touch forks jq) — NOT for log streams; bench publishes honest numbers before claims. Workflow: phase -> dual-bash -> master sweep -> STOP -> 'go'; commits gated; P0 ends in a MANDATORY owner review gate.

baseline.measured: 2026-07-12 — at roadmap planning time (post-tdictionary). RE-MEASURE at P0: roadmap units 1-6 will have raised the totals substantially.

suites_green.kcl_breakdown: tcustomapplication 320, tdirectory 197, tfile 120, tlist 92, tpath 112, tstringhelper 393, tstringlist 155, dateutils 123, math 81, tdictionary 130

baseline.regression_rule: fpjson work must keep the (re-measured) baseline green; the new suite adds on top.

design_decisions.node_model_RECOMMENDED: OWNER DECISION #1 at P0. Recommended (B): ONE kklass document instance + integer node handles into parallel per-doc arrays (${doc}_type/_value/_children/_names + free-counter). Rejected by arithmetic (A): kklass instance per node (~ms/ctor x 10k nodes = tens of seconds). Fallback (C): path-only API (O(depth) per access, awkward mutation). Child IDs join on \x1f (integers — always safe); member NAMES live in per-id scalar slots, NEVER joined (JSON names may contain any char incl. \x1f).
design_decisions.engine_RECOMMENDED: OWNER DECISION #2 at P0. Recommended: pure-bash lexer/parser first (Tier A, zero deps, exact positioned errors); P0 probe publishes REAL byte-loop throughput numbers before commitment. Optional P8 (own gate): awk-coprocess tokenizer behind the SAME token interface (math-engine precedent: one fork per process, sub-ms round-trips; tempfile program feed + TMPDIR fallback lessons apply) — only if real kkbot usage needs it; corpus-equivalence-tested against the pure lexer.
design_decisions.parser_structure: Iterative explicit-stack state machine (bash recursion is slow and stack-risky at JSON depths). Atomic errors: any parse failure leaves the document EMPTY, rc=1, RESULT_ERROR='line L col C: message' (positioned errors are a hard usability requirement).
design_decisions.numbers: STRING-PRESERVING: one number kind + int/float subtype tag; literals survive parse->serialize byte-exact (FPC collapses through Double — documented divergence). AsInteger = bash 64-bit with range check; AsFloat = validated passthrough; arithmetic belongs to kcl/math. FPC's five number classes collapse (no-parity-theater rule).
design_decisions.serialization: AsJSON matches FPC byte-for-byte (FPC emits spaced style — exact convention pinned at P0 from DoJSON impls) so fixtures can be literal; FormatJSON option subset pinned at P0; output emits raw UTF-8 (no \u escaping) matching FPC default.
design_decisions.unicode: Storage = raw UTF-8 bytes; lexer runs a scoped LC_ALL=C BYTE loop (O(1) ${s:i:1} — THE perf idiom, probed both bashes); \uXXXX decode via printf (probed), surrogate pairs composed manually, \u0000 -> parse error (bash NUL limit), lone surrogates per FPC (S7 pin).
design_decisions.insertion_order: TJSONObject preserves insertion order (FPHashList) — pinned and kept: names order array per object node; serialization and iteration follow it.

goals: Strict RFC 8259 parse with FPC-pinned dialect decisions (S1-S12), positioned errors, atomic failure.
Byte-parity serialization vs FPC convention; parse->AsJSON->parse fixpoint; number-literal preservation.
JSONTestSuite y_/n_ subset green (i_ decided + documented); fcl-json fpcunit seeds ported.
Zero forks in the whole pipeline (pure-bash v1); honest bench (1/10/100 KB) before any performance claim.
Handle-based API that scales to 10k-node documents without per-node instance cost.

test_basis.seed_primary: fcl-json/tests/testjson.pp (fpcunit data-model tests: Add/Delete/FindPath/AsXxx) — mined at P0.
seed_additional: JSONTestSuite (seriot.ch) y_/n_/i_ subset — vendored into tests/fixtures with provenance notes; NUL-bearing cases become expected-reject rows.
Roundtrip/property tests: fixpoint, literal preservation, order stability, Clone independence.

parity_model.tier_A_exact: Parse acceptance/rejection, tree shapes, getter coercions, serialization bytes, path resolution, mutation semantics — byte-for-byte vs FPC-pinned behavior.
parity_model.corpus: JSONTestSuite verdicts exact; i_ cases get a decided, documented, tested behavior.

test_basis.new_coverage_protocol: Non-FPC cases -> TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md rows; corpus rows cite JSONTestSuite ids.

out_of_scope.item: Five number classes (Float/Integer/Int64/NativeInt/QWord :231-393) as distinct types
out_of_scope.reason: One literal-preserving number kind + subtype tag; no Double roundtrip, no float engine. Documented divergence (strictly MORE faithful to the input text than FPC).
No streams in bash; ParseFile reads whole files. SAX-style is the P8 coproc's possible future, not v1.
Separate FPC units; CompareJSON noted as a possible later extra.
bash strings are UTF-8 bytes; one string kind.
bash language limit; parse error with a clear positioned message.

phases.name: Design study + OWNER REVIEW + probes
phases.goal: Pin S1-S12 from impls; probe \u printf, byte-loop throughput (real numbers), LC_ALL scoping; present node-model and engine options WITH measurements; owner picks; amend plan; skeleton + runner; baseline re-measure.

tasks.title: Semantics pin S1-S12 (dup keys, insertion order, AsJSON bytes, FormatJSON options, FindPath grammar, scanner strictness, surrogates, coercions, Clone, number edges, depth guard, null-vs-missing) with line refs; mine testjson.pp; vendor corpus subset
tasks.acceptance: S-table complete; corpus fixture list committed with provenance.

tasks.title: Probes: printf \u + surrogate composition on BOTH bashes; LC_ALL=C byte-loop throughput measurement (chars/sec -> projected KB parse times); scoping semantics
tasks.acceptance: Real numbers recorded here — they inform the engine decision.

tasks.title: OWNER REVIEW: node model (A/B/C) + engine (pure vs +coproc-P8) presented with measurements; decisions recorded; plan amended; skeleton + runner + baseline re-measure
tasks.acceptance: Both decisions logged in design_decisions with owner sign-off date; DRAFT flag removed from plan.

phases.name: Lexer

phases.name: Parser -> document storage
phases.goal: Explicit-stack parser; atomic errors; dup-key rule; corpus acceptance.

tasks.title: Iterative parser into handle storage; atomic failure (empty doc + RESULT_ERROR line:col); S1 dup rule; y_/n_ corpus
tasks.acceptance: Corpus verdicts exact; atomicity proven; dual-bash; sweep gate.

tasks.title: Path resolution per S5 grammar + typed getters per S8 coercions + S12 null-vs-missing + ForEach snapshot
tasks.acceptance: testjson.pp seed parity green; exotic member names; dual-bash; sweep gate.

tasks.title: AsJSON (S3 bytes) + FormatJSON (S4 subset) + fixpoint + literal-preservation + order-stability proofs
tasks.acceptance: Byte-compare fixtures green; roundtrip fixpoint on corpus; dual-bash; sweep gate.

tasks.title: Typed add families + deletes + Clear + Clone (S9) + order preservation under mutation + handle recycling policy
tasks.acceptance: Mutation matrix green; serialize-after-mutate exact; dual-bash; sweep gate.

phases.name: Hardening
phases.goal: Full corpus + pathological inputs + error-position matrix.

phases.goal: README + docs/TJSONData.md + bench + coverage notes + final sweep.

tasks.title: README (API, positioning, jq-vs-fpjson guidance) + docs/TJSONData.md (upstream FPC family reference per kcl docs convention)

bench.sh (parse 1/10/100 KB, GetPath hot, AsJSON, informational jq-fork comparison), timed via TStopwatch.getTimeStamp (shared kcl bench clock — source ../tstopwatch/tstopwatch.sh; do NOT hand-roll _now_us) + TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES finalization + final sweep, ledger COMPLETE

phases.goal: Same token interface, corpus-equivalent, math-engine lifecycle lessons; ONLY on owner demand after v1 usage.

tasks.title: Coproc tokenizer (tempfile program feed, TMPDIR->MATH_DIR-style fallback, lazy start, $() subshell caveat documented) + full-corpus equivalence vs pure lexer + bench delta
tasks.acceptance: Equivalence 100% on corpus; bench delta recorded; graceful degradation without awk.

execution_log.summary: fpjson PLAN.md (DRAFT) + ledger written; folders created. No implementation. Architecture forks explicitly reserved for the P0 owner review (node model: document-instance+handles recommended — object-per-node rejected by ctor-cost arithmetic; engine: pure-bash first recommended, awk-coproc as optional gated P8 per the math-engine precedent). Pinned planning decisions: string-preserving numbers (one kind + subtype tag), iterative explicit-stack parser with atomic positioned errors, LC_ALL=C byte-loop lexer idiom, insertion-order objects, JSONTestSuite subset as adversarial corpus, \u0000 documented-reject.
